body_txt: Because Same Surname Means Related , an entire fictional family will always share the same surname, even grandparents, aunts, uncles, cousins, etc. Even in cases where it wouldn't make sense, most often with relatives through their mother's side of the family. If writers want to attempt to be realistic by avoiding this discrepancy, they'll make it a Single Gender Family with all blood relatives sans the matriarch being male because Lineage Comes from the Father . Any aunts are either an aunt-by-marriage or a Maiden Aunt . First cousins almost always share a surname, indicating that they would be the father's brother's kids note That is, assuming the brothers are full siblings or at least share the same father. . Now extended relatives beyond that, such as great-grandparents, great aunts/uncles, cousins once removed, second/third/fourth cousins, etc. will almost always still share the same surname, indicating that they're all related through the male bloodline! note For example, second cousins will be the father's father's father's brother's son's kids Of course, that's not to say maternal relatives don't exist in fiction. As far as surnames go for them, it could come in multiple flavors: the character's father is out of the picture and thus shares their mother's maiden name; the character's father Took the Wife's Name (which is often implied); or their mother's maiden name is unknown thus the maternal relatives are only referred to by their first names or nicknames. If it's the third option, this allows for freedom for the writers to make relatives of any sex without worrying about discrepancies. In the cases that the mother's surname is known, she almost always will still have brothers with kids rather than sisters, as well as great-grandparents or great-aunts/uncles (or other extended relatives) being through her father's side. Both of these options make it easier for the writers to only have to keep track of one or two surnames for the sake of convenience and simplicity. And in some cases, although rare, a woman's surname may be the same as her maiden name due to the fact that Same Surname Means Related isn't always true and there are plenty of people who share a surname that aren't related. Thus, she may have happened to marry a man with the same surname. Note that this is not the same as Same Surname Means Related , as that trope is about the fact that characters will only share a surname if they are related, while this one is the other way around in that characters will only be related if they share a surname. Often paired with Single Line of Descent . May be justified in a Tangled Family Tree , especially if it involves incest. The Three Successful Generations trio often all share the same surname (typically being son, father, and grandfather). Compare Lineage Comes from the Father and Ridiculously Long-lived Family Name . See also Nom de Mom , Took the Wife's Name , and The Maiden Name Debate . Species Surname might be a reason for subversions and other plays.
